title: Take The Employed Route, Add Email Updates, And Reach The Result
goal: Follow the employed route to check answers on the no-email branch, add email updates from the summary, and continue to the result page.
instruction: Complete the childcare-support eligibility prototype on the employed route without email updates, then change updates to Yes, add casey.benefits@example.com, and stop on the final eligibility result page.
review_notes: Hard end-to-end benefits task that adds the email branch from check answers and continues through to the result page.

success_assertions.value: You may be able to continue with a full childcare-support application.

steps.explanation: I selected Yes because the user usually lives in the UK.
The residency answer is complete, so I am moving to the childcare question.
I selected one child for the childcare count.
The childcare answer is complete, so I am moving to work status.
I selected Employed as the current work status.
The work status is complete, so I am moving to income.
I entered the monthly income for the eligibility estimate.
The income is complete, so I am moving to updates.
I started on the no-email branch so the summary initially has no email row.
The initial branch is complete, so I am moving to check answers.
I followed the updates change link so I can add the email branch before continuing to the result.
I switched the updates answer to Yes, which reveals the email field.
I entered the email address for updates.
The added email branch is complete, so I am returning to review before the result page.
I continued from the review page to the final eligibility result with the added email branch.

postconditions.value: You may be able to continue with a full childcare-support application.

action.summary: I completed the employed route, added email updates from check answers, and reached the positive eligibility result page.

steps.explanation: The final result page is visible with the expected employed-route wording and retained email state, so I am ending the task.
